section.heading: Protection of transmission contracts in the Pacific Northwest

subsection.heading: Definition of electric utility or person
subsection.chapeau: In this section, the term “electric utility or person” means an electric utility or person that—

paragraph.content: as of August 8, 2005, holds firm transmission rights pursuant to contract or by reason of ownership of transmission facilities; and

subparagraph.content: in the Pacific Northwest, as that region is defined in section 839a of this title; or
in that portion of a State included in the geographic area proposed for a regional transmission organization in Commission Docket Number RT01–35 on the date on which that docket was opened.

subsection.heading: Protection of transmission contracts
subsection.chapeau: Nothing in this chapter confers on the Commission the authority to require an electric utility or person to convert to tradable or financial rights—

paragraph.content: firm transmission rights described in subsection (a); or
firm transmission rights obtained by exercising contract or tariff rights associated with the firm transmission rights described in subsection (a).

section.sourceCredit: (June 10, 1920, ch. 285, pt. II, § 218, as added Pub. L. 109–58, title XII, § 1235, Aug. 8, 2005, 119 Stat. 960.)
